title: Bitcoin
topicalAnchor: Bitcoin — the monetary thesis, the protocol's actual mechanics, and the self-custody stack
suggestedPaths: Interest rates and the cost of money

edges.label: Austrian monetary thought
edges.summary: Sound money, hard money, the regression theorem, inflation and malinvestment, why a fixed supply matters. The intellectual heritage that the monetary case for Bitcoin draws on — Mises, Rothbard, Hayek, Hülsmann.

edges.label: Bitcoin Core internals
edges.summary: How the reference client actually works under the hood: UTXO set, transaction validation, mempool policy, the consensus rules, block templates, the P2P network, initial block download. What a node does on every block.

edges.label: Lightning Network
edges.summary: Payment channels, HTLCs, routing across the channel graph, watchtowers, splicing, BOLT specifications. How off-chain settlement preserves Bitcoin's security model while moving billions of micropayments.

edges.label: On-chain privacy and chain analysis
edges.summary: Address reuse, coinjoin, payjoin, silent payments, what blockchain surveillance firms can and can't see, taproot's privacy implications. Practical and philosophical takes on financial privacy.

edges.label: Wallets, keys, and self-custody
edges.summary: HD wallets, seed phrases, descriptors, hardware wallets, multisig, inheritance planning, how to actually hold your own keys without losing everything in a closet flood. Sovereignty in practice.

edges.label: Mining and proof of work
edges.summary: Why proof of work, ASICs and the centralization debate, mining pool dynamics, difficulty adjustment, the security budget question, energy use in context. The economics and engineering of producing blocks.

edges.label: The whitepaper and its precursors
edges.summary: Satoshi's original 2008 paper and the intellectual lineage it pulls from — Hashcash, b-money, Szabo's Bit Gold, Hal Finney's RPOW. Where the ideas actually came from and what each predecessor got right.

edges.label: Operational how-to
edges.summary: Real-world questions: setting up a node, choosing a hardware wallet, how to receive payments without a custodian, backup procedures, inheritance planning, what to do when something goes wrong. Practical, not philosophical.
